skill.name: rn-async-patterns
skill.description: Guidance for finding and fixing async/await bugs in React Native and Expo apps that use Zustand state management.
skill.summary: Review React Native async flows for missing awaits, race conditions, post-condition checks, and useEffect timing issues.
skill.author: CJHarmath

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown backticks and code examples as external command execution, plus two high-severity keyword matches. Review found a documentation-only skill with no scripts, no runnable package files, no network behavior, and no prompt injection attempt. The findings are false positives, with a low residual risk because the skill suggests grep commands for local review.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged external command locations are Markdown inline code, TypeScript examples, JSON examples, or grep snippets used to teach async code review. The skill contains no executable scripts or package hooks. The only shell examples run local grep searches and do not execute untrusted input, access secrets, or send data externally.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is a Markdown skill guide, and the flagged lines are fenced examples or inline formatting. No separate executable file, package script, network call, or command construction was found in the provided file set.

low_findings.title: Certificate Or Key File Match Is A Keyword False Positive
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The scanner flagged line 61, but this line checks Object.keys in a TypeScript validation example. It does not contain a certificate, private key, token, or credential material.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 61 uses Object.keys to count loaded assessment answers. The semantic context is state validation, not secret storage or credential handling.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Match Is A Keyword False Positive
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The scanner flagged line 3, which is the frontmatter description for async/await correctness. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, encryption code, or security-sensitive primitive appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is plain metadata describing React Native async debugging. There is no cryptographic API or algorithm reference to evaluate.

content.value_statement: Async timing bugs in React Native can cause race conditions, stale Zustand state, and missed failures. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a focused checklist for reviewing async flows and suggesting safer patterns.
actual_capabilities: Identifies missing await usage and floating promises in React Native TypeScript examples.
Explains when async operations should run sequentially or with Promise.all.
Provides post-condition validation patterns after critical state-loading operations.
Reviews async useEffect patterns with cleanup guards for unmounted components.
Suggests ESLint rules that catch common promise and await mistakes.
limitations: It is a guidance-only skill and does not include an automated analyzer.
The grep examples are broad and can produce false positives.
It focuses on React Native, Expo, Zustand, and TypeScript codebases.
It does not replace runtime testing on slow devices or poor networks.

use_cases.title: Debug a Retake Flow Race Condition
use_cases.description: Review a React Native flow where state updates and answer clearing run in the wrong order.

use_cases.title: Prepare an Async Code Review
use_cases.description: Create a checklist for pull requests that change promise handling, Zustand actions, or useEffect data loading.

use_cases.title: Harden Critical Mobile Actions
use_cases.description: Add validation after loading, saving, submitting, or deleting data before later steps depend on that state.

prompt_templates.title: Find Missing Awaits
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one file or small diff.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React Native TypeScript code for missing await usage and floating promises. Explain each risk and suggest the smallest safe fix.

prompt_templates.title: Check Zustand Async Actions
prompt_templates.scenario: Review state actions that call async helpers.
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect these Zustand actions for hidden async behavior, race conditions, and state updates that should be awaited or validated.

prompt_templates.title: Audit useEffect Data Loading
prompt_templates.scenario: Review component lifecycle and cleanup behavior.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these useEffect blocks for async callback mistakes, stale updates after unmount, missing cleanup guards, and dependency timing risks.

prompt_templates.title: Design Async Sequencing Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of a multi-step mobile workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this workflow and classify each async operation as sequential or parallel. Identify required post-condition checks and failure handling.

output_examples.input: A retake workflow calls a state setup action and then clears answers.
output_examples.output: The review flags that the setup action must complete before answer clearing starts. It recommends awaiting the setup step and validating loaded state before continuing.

output_examples.input: A component loads remote data inside useEffect and updates local state.
output_examples.output: The review recommends moving async work into an inner function, adding a cancellation guard, and checking dependencies for stale updates.

output_examples.input: A pull request changes several promise chains to Promise.all.
output_examples.output: The review separates independent operations from state-dependent operations and recommends sequential awaits where shared state order matters.

best_practices: Enable TypeScript ESLint promise rules before relying on manual review.
Validate critical post-conditions after loading or mutating shared state.
Document intentional fire-and-forget calls with clear error handling.
anti_patterns: Calling async actions without await when later code depends on their result.
Using Promise.all for operations that mutate or read shared state in sequence.
Making a useEffect callback async instead of defining an inner async function.

faq.question: Does this skill run code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides review guidance and example search commands, but it does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Which projects fit this skill best?
faq.answer: It fits React Native or Expo apps that use TypeScript and Zustand for state management.

faq.question: Can it find every async bug?
faq.answer: No. It helps structure review, but runtime tests are still needed for timing and device-specific failures.

faq.question: Why does it discuss post-condition validation?
faq.answer: Async calls can fail or update state later than expected. Validation confirms required state before the workflow continues.

faq.question: Does it support Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The prompts and review checklist can guide Claude, Codex, and Claude Code during code review.

faq.question: Are the grep commands required?
faq.answer: No. They are optional examples for local searching and should be reviewed before use in each repository.
